deduplication.method: canonical URL + normalized title + event/company/date + syndicated text check

sources.title: Introducing Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform, powering the next wave of agents
sources.author: Michael Gerstenhaber
keyPoints: Google launched Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform as a new place to build, scale, govern, and optimize agents.
The platform folds model selection, model building, agent building, orchestration, DevOps, and security into one stack.
It adds Agent Studio, Agent Runtime, Agent Registry, Agent Gateway, simulation, evaluation, and observability tools.
Google says all Vertex AI roadmap changes will move through this platform.

sources.title: What’s new with compute: Scaling core and agentic workloads
sources.author: Nirav Mehta
keyPoints: Google announced compute updates aimed at both core workloads and agentic AI workloads.
The post highlights GKE Agent Sandbox, Axion N4A, C4A.metal preview, C4N preview, M4N preview, Z4D, and flexible CUD expansion.
Google frames static infrastructure as a bottleneck when agent demand spikes suddenly.
Customer examples point to latency, cost, and isolation pressure in AI and general-purpose systems.

sources.title: Introducing Virgo Network, Google’s scale-out AI data center fabric
sources.author: Benny Siman-Tov
keyPoints: Google described Virgo Network as a new megascale AI data center fabric for the AI era.
The architecture targets bandwidth, latency, and fault-isolation problems in large model training.
Google says Virgo can link 134,000 chips with up to 47 petabits per second of non-blocking bandwidth.
The post emphasizes observability and straggler/hang detection for reliability at scale.

sources.title: Introducing Google Cloud Fraud Defense, the next evolution of reCAPTCHA
sources.author: Jian Zhen
keyPoints: Google launched Fraud Defense as a trust platform for the agentic web.
The product adds agentic activity measurement, an agentic policy engine, and a QR-code human verification challenge.
Google says existing reCAPTCHA customers are automatically included with no migration required.
The launch frames AI-agent traffic, bot abuse, and synthetic identity fraud as a growing enterprise risk.

sources.title: Google Cloud and SAP unveil blueprint for the Agentic Enterprise
sources.author: Casey McGee
keyPoints: Google Cloud and SAP announced a deeper partnership around Gemini and enterprise workflows.
The update centers on a Unified Data Foundation and zero-copy data sharing between SAP and non-SAP data.
The post says SAP Engagement Cloud is using Google Cloud to build agentic marketing and content tools.
The launch positions data movement and enterprise context as core constraints for agentic applications.

signals.title: Agent platforms are being packaged as one stack
signals.summary: Google’s Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform bundles model access, orchestration, runtime, governance, and evaluation, signaling demand for a full agent platform rather than point tools.

signals.title: Agent workloads need their own infra primitives
signals.summary: Compute, sandboxing, and network announcements show that agentic workloads are exposing new bottlenecks around isolation, latency, and burst scaling.

signals.title: Trust and identity for AI traffic is becoming a product category
signals.summary: Fraud Defense treats bot, human, and agent verification as a distinct layer, suggesting buyers now need controls for autonomous web traffic.

signals.title: Zero-copy enterprise data access is becoming table stakes
signals.summary: The SAP partnership frames data movement and enterprise context as a blocker for agentic workflows, pointing to demand for direct, governed data access.

signalMap.title: Today’s startup signals around agentic enterprise infrastructure
signalMap.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  S1[Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform] --> SIG1[Agent platform consolidation]
  S2[Compute + Virgo launches] --> SIG2[Infra bottlenecks for agents]
  S3[Fraud Defense] --> SIG3[Trust layer for autonomous traffic]
  S4[SAP zero-copy partnership] --> SIG4[Zero-copy enterprise data access]
  SIG1 --> O1[Unified agent stack]
  SIG2 --> O2[Specialized AI infrastructure]
  SIG3 --> O3[Identity and fraud controls]
  SIG4 --> O4[Governed enterprise data plumbing]

signalRefs: Agent platforms are being packaged as one stack
Agent workloads need their own infra primitives
Trust and identity for AI traffic is becoming a product category
Zero-copy enterprise data access is becoming table stakes

gaps: Most verified sources in the window came from Google Cloud Next '26 launch posts; I did not find enough independent non-Google sources within the same date window that met the startup-signal filter.
